title: Rich Moroccan Almond Amlou
description: A traditional Moroccan dessert made with almonds, honey, and unwaxed lemon, featuring a winey and crumbly texture, this recipe is a nostalgic treat for the senses. It is also gluten-free-friendly and incorporates kombu-cold-extract for added depth.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 350 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, mix together the sliced almonds, honey, unwaxed lemon zest, and kombu-cold-extract until well combined.
Add the argan oil, orange blossom water, and sea salt to the bowl and mix until a crumbly dough forms.
Transfer the dough to a parchment-lined baking sheet and shape into a rectangular form.
Bake for 20 minutes or until the edges are lightly golden.
Remove from the oven and let cool completely on the baking sheet.
Once cool, break the amlou into small pieces and serve.
Store any leftovers in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 5 days.
